skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: Helps draft internal communications such as 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQ answers, status reports, leadership updates, and incident updates.
skill.summary: Draft structured internal company communications from user-provided or authorized workplace context.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static findings for weak cryptography, command execution, and hardcoded URLs were false positives from prose, Markdown file references, and standard Apache License URLs. No prompt injection, malware behavior, executable code, or external exfiltration was found. The remaining concern is medium risk because the skill encourages broad use of authorized Slack, email, calendar, and document sources that may contain sensitive internal information.

medium_findings.title: Broad Internal Data Collection Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill directs assistants to use workplace sources such as Slack, email, calendar, and documents when available. This is legitimate for internal communications, but users must enforce permissions, minimize collection, and avoid including confidential content beyond the target audience.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited guidance explicitly recommends collecting information from internal collaboration and document systems. This is not malicious, but it creates a clear data handling risk if tool access is broader than the user's audience or authorization.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches in Prose
low_findings.description: Static weak cryptography alerts matched ordinary words in descriptions, communication examples, and Apache License text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, key handling, or security-sensitive implementation is present at the cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of each cited line shows natural language or license boilerplate, not source code. There is no evidence of weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Are Not Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick alerts are Markdown formatting around example file paths in the usage instructions. They do not execute commands and do not accept user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks wrap names such as examples/company-newsletter.md inside a Markdown list. There is no Ruby code, shell syntax, script, or execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: License URLs Are Not Runtime Network Calls
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are standard Apache License reference links. They are static license text and do not cause any network request during skill use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs appear in the Apache License text. No code references these URLs, and no runtime networking behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Internal updates often lose clarity when teams use inconsistent formats and scattered source material. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft structured updates, newsletters, and FAQ answers from authorized context.
actual_capabilities: Drafts concise 3P updates with Progress, Plans, and Problems sections.
Creates company newsletter bullets for Slack and email distribution.
Formats internal FAQ questions and answers from company context.
Guides the assistant to ask for audience, purpose, tone, and format when needed.
Suggests using authorized Slack, email, calendar, and document context when available.
Maintains short, direct, and audience-aware internal communication style.
limitations: Does not include scripts, integrations, or direct connectors to workplace tools.
Requires user-provided content or separately authorized tool access.
Does not verify facts unless source material is provided or accessible.
May need human review for confidential, legal, executive, or incident communications.

use_cases.title: Prepare Weekly Team 3P Updates
use_cases.description: Turn team progress, next plans, and blockers into a short executive-readable update.

use_cases.title: Draft Company Newsletter Bullets
use_cases.description: Summarize major company announcements, leadership notes, milestones, and external recognition for Slack or email.

use_cases.title: Answer Common Employee Questions
use_cases.description: Create concise FAQ responses based on official announcements, documents, or user-provided context.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic 3P Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the internal-comms skill to write a 3P update for the Support team. Progress: resolved 120 tickets and launched new macros. Plans: improve escalation docs next week. Problems: staffing is tight during morning coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Polish a Leadership Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the internal-comms skill to rewrite this leadership update for executives. Keep it concise, data-driven, and direct. Ask me for missing audience or timing details before drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Company Newsletter
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the internal-comms skill to draft a company newsletter for this month. Group the update into clear sections and include only company-wide items from the notes I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Internal FAQ
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the internal-comms skill to create an internal FAQ about the new operating plan. Base answers only on the official notes below, mark uncertain answers clearly, and flag questions needing executive review.

output_examples.input: Write a 3P update for the Data team using weekly notes.
output_examples.output:
📊 Data Team (June 22 to June 29)
Progress: We shipped the new retention dashboard and reduced reporting latency by 35%.
Plans: We will validate finance metrics and prepare the self-serve analytics rollout.
Problems: Two partner tables still have missing owner mappings, which may delay final QA.

output_examples.input: Draft newsletter bullets from company-wide announcements.
output_examples.output:
Company Announcements
- We launched the new onboarding hub for all new hires.
- Leadership shared the Q3 operating priorities after the all-hands meeting.

Progress on Priorities
- The product team completed the beta rollout for three strategic customers.
- Recruiting opened two roles tied to customer implementation capacity.

output_examples.input: Create FAQ answers about a new policy.
output_examples.output:
Question: When does the new travel policy start?
Answer: The policy starts on July 1. Employees should use the updated approval workflow for all new bookings.

Question: Who approves exceptions?
Answer: Exceptions require manager approval and review by the finance operations team.

best_practices: Provide the target audience, time period, source notes, and intended channel before drafting.
Use only authorized internal sources and remove confidential details that the audience should not see.
Ask a human owner to review facts, tone, and sensitive claims before distribution.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the assistant to search private systems without clear authorization and purpose.
Do not include rumors, uncertain claims, or sensitive employee information in broad updates.
Do not use the newsletter format for detailed team-only project tracking.

faq.question: What types of internal communications does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQ answers, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, and incident updates.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to Slack, email, or Google Drive by itself?
faq.answer: No. It only provides writing guidance. Any workplace tool access must come from the user's environment and permissions.

faq.question: Can it write updates from raw notes?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide notes, metrics, dates, blockers, and audience details, and it can convert them into the requested format.

faq.question: How should sensitive information be handled?
faq.answer: Use only authorized sources, minimize copied details, and remove confidential information that is not appropriate for the audience.

faq.question: Can it create company-wide newsletters?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize major announcements, leadership updates, milestones, and external recognition into concise newsletter bullets.

faq.question: Does it replace communications review?
faq.answer: No. Human review is recommended for factual accuracy, tone, confidentiality, legal issues, and executive messaging.
